news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces First Patient Dosed in Phase 2 Clinical Trial of HEPZATO in Liver-Dominant Metastatic Breast Cancer
news.summary: Delcath dosed the first patient, at the European Institute of Oncology in Milan, in its global randomized Phase 2 DELUMA trial testing HEPZATO plus standard of care versus standard of care alone in liver-dominant HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer. The roughly 90-patient trial is planned to run at more than 20 sites across the US and Europe.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces Two ASCO 2026 Investigator Initiated Trials-in-Progress Abstracts
news.summary: Two investigator-initiated trial-in-progress abstracts combining Delcath's percutaneous hepatic perfusion (PHP) with newer systemic therapies -- sequential PHP followed by tebentafusp in metastatic uveal melanoma, and PHP plus nivolumab/relatlimab in liver-metastatic melanoma -- were accepted as posters at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting in Chicago. The studies aim to show how liver-directed PHP can be sequenced or combined with modern immunotherapy regimens.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Reports First Quarter 2026 Results and Business Highlights
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue rose to $25.0 million from $19.8 million a year earlier, driven by roughly 36% growth in HEPZATO KIT volume and four new center activations, and the company reiterated full-year 2026 revenue guidance of at least $100 million. Delcath ended the quarter with $89.3 million in cash and a narrow $1.1 million net loss.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces Presentation of New Data on Percutaneous Hepatic Perfusion with Melphalan in Liver-Dominant Metastatic Breast Cancer at ESMO Breast Cancer 2026
news.summary: Independent European investigators presented a retrospective analysis of 15 liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer patients treated with Delcath's CHEMOSAT (M-PHP) at the ESMO Breast Cancer 2026 congress in Berlin, reporting hepatic partial responses in 9 of 15 patients. The data helped build the rationale behind the newly launched Phase 2 DELUMA breast cancer trial.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces Inclusion of CHEMOSAT Hepatic Delivery System for Melphalan as a Recommended Liver-Directed Regional Therapy Option in the ESMO-EURACAN Clinical Practice Guidelines for Uveal Melanoma
news.summary: Delcath's CHEMOSAT Hepatic Delivery System was named the first-listed regional treatment option for multifocal liver-only uveal melanoma metastases in the newly published ESMO-EURACAN Clinical Practice Guideline. The guideline cites two Phase 3 trials showing improved hepatic and overall progression-free survival versus best alternative care.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces Publication of CHOPIN Clinical Trial Results in The Lancet Oncology
news.summary: Full results from the investigator-initiated, randomized Phase 2 CHOPIN trial -- combining Delcath's PHP with ipilimumab and nivolumab in metastatic uveal melanoma -- were published in The Lancet Oncology, showing more than triple the 1-year progression-free survival rate and nearly double the 2-year overall survival versus PHP alone. The trial was led by Professor Ellen Kapiteijn of Leiden University Medical Center and builds on topline data first presented at ESMO 2025.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly results are expected roughly three months after the May 7, 2026 Q1 print (Q2 2025's report landed August 6); no specific Q2 2026 call date had been announced as of July 16, 2026. Management will likely update HEPZATO volume growth, site-activation pace and progress toward the $100M+ FY2026 revenue guidance.

catalysts.label: Full-year 2026 revenue guidance of at least $100 million
catalysts.detail: Reiterated on the Q1 2026 call, implying at least 20% growth in HEPZATO KIT volume over 2025; delivery against this target is a key near-term proof point.

catalysts.label: HEPZATO active treatment center expansion to ~40 sites
catalysts.detail: Management guided to 37 active US centers by year-end 2026 and 40 by Q1 2027 (slower than earlier targets), a key driver of continued HEPZATO volume growth.

catalysts.label: Phase 2 DELUMA trial in liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer
catalysts.detail: First patient was dosed July 9, 2026 in the ~90-patient randomized trial of HEPZATO plus standard of care vs. standard of care alone; enrollment across 20+ US/European sites is the near-term milestone, with efficacy data still to come.

catalysts.label: Phase 2 trial in liver-dominant metastatic colorectal cancer
catalysts.detail: Randomized ~90-patient study of HEPZATO plus trifluridine-tipiracil/bevacizumab vs. that regimen alone in third-line colorectal cancer patients (about 8 of a planned ~26 sites activated as of mid-2026, with the remainder targeted for activation by mid-2026). Primary endpoint (hepatic progression-free survival) is anticipated around mid-2028, with overall survival data expected in late 2028.

pulse: Delcath's commercial ramp continues: Q1 2026 revenue grew to $25.0 million on ~36% HEPZATO volume growth, and management reiterated full-year 2026 guidance of at least $100 million in revenue. The clinical pipeline keeps broadening beyond melanoma -- the first patient is now dosed in the Phase 2 DELUMA breast cancer trial, alongside an ongoing colorectal cancer Phase 2 study, growing guideline recognition (ESMO-EURACAN) for CHEMOSAT in uveal melanoma, and a full Lancet Oncology publication of the positive CHOPIN combination-therapy data. News flow has been unusually quiet through the rest of June and into mid-July following the July 9 DELUMA announcement -- no further press releases, financing, or trial updates have surfaced -- with the stock trading in the roughly $11-14 range near its 52-week high heading into the next earnings report, which analysts expect in early-to-mid August but which has not yet been formally scheduled.
